mappings.notes: Injecting malicious content into agent persistent memory or RAG stores
Crafting content whose embeddings bias future retrieval results in attacker's favour
Establishing persistent trigger-response patterns in agent memory stores
Agent memory treated as sensitive data asset — classification, access controls, retention policy
Memory poisoning risk mapped per agent — memory stores, trust levels, access controls, TTL documented
Adversarial testing of memory integrity — poisoning scenarios, anomaly detection effectiveness
Response for confirmed memory poisoning — audit procedure, content purge, operational impact assessment

mappings.control_id: Data governance applies to all data influencing AI behaviour — including agent memory
mappings.notes: Agent memory governance — classification, access controls, integrity validation, retention — is an Art. 10 requirement

mappings.control_id: Technical resilience against adversarial memory manipulation
mappings.notes: Memory integrity monitoring and access controls are Art. 15 requirements
Memory anomaly detection in post-market monitoring programme
Access controls on all agent memory stores — only agent and designated administrators can write
Agent memory stores encrypted at rest — embeddings, long-term memory, operational knowledge base
Memory store access and content monitored — anomalous write patterns, statistical integrity checks
DLP on memory write paths — credential patterns, sensitive content detected before memory write
Agent memory content quality requirements — access controls, integrity verification, TTL as data quality controls
Memory provenance tracked — source, write access controls, TTL, modification history in AIMS
Memory store access controls and integrity monitoring as AIMS security design requirements
Memory integrity monitored in operation — anomalous write patterns, content integrity checks as AIMS monitoring
Agent memory stores encrypted at rest — embeddings, long-term memory, operational knowledge
Memory write operations logged — who or what wrote, when, content hash
Memory write validation as secure development requirement — untrusted sources cannot write directly

mappings.control_id: 13.3 Deploy a network-based intrusion detection system
mappings.notes: Memory store access monitored — bulk read, anomalous write patterns detected

mappings.control_name: Verify all user input validated against allowlist
mappings.notes: All content entering agent memory validated — injection patterns rejected at write boundary

mappings.control_name: Verify access control enforces least privilege
mappings.notes: Memory write access restricted to minimum required sources — only agent and authorised administrators can write

mappings.control_name: Verify sensitive data encrypted at rest
mappings.notes: Agent memory stores encrypted at rest — embeddings, long-term memory
Content entering agent memory scanned — adversarial content, injection patterns detected before write
Agent memory content validated for integrity — unauthorised modifications detected
Continuous monitoring of agent memory for anomalous content patterns or unexpected modifications
Memory poisoning indicators treated as security events — agent suspended, memory audited, human notified
Agent memory baseline established and maintained — deviations from baseline detectable
OT data exfiltration via compromised automation
Assess outbound data paths from agent
Monitor all outbound data from OT zone
Agent memory stores protected at rest — access controls, encryption, integrity verification
Monitoring for anomalous data — memory content integrity checks, unusual write patterns detected
Agent memory stores inventoried as data assets — content classification, access controls, TTL documented
Memory poisoning incidents analysed — affected sessions identified, operational decisions influenced reviewed

mappings.control_id: Memory poisoning risk documented in risk assessment — scenarios for RAG, persistent memory, and context window corruption
mappings.notes: Risk register with memory poisoning entries

mappings.control_id: Agent memory stores monitored for unexpected modifications — baseline established, deviations trigger alerts
mappings.notes: Memory access log, anomaly alert configuration

mappings.control_id: Processing based on compromised memory is not authorised — memory integrity controls support PI1 evidence
mappings.notes: Integrity verification configuration

mappings.control_id: Controls on memory store write access — only authorised processes may write to agent memory stores
mappings.notes: Memory access policy, write access controls

mappings.control_id: PAN in agent memory protected — primary account numbers truncated or masked if stored in agent context or memory stores
mappings.notes: Memory store review, PAN protection evidence

mappings.control_id: Agent memory encryption — CHD in persistent memory stores encrypted using strong cryptography
mappings.notes: Encryption configuration, key management records

mappings.control_id: Model and memory changes managed — updates to agent memory stores treated as system changes requiring security review
mappings.notes: Change management records

mappings.control_id: Memory poisoning in penetration test scope — test whether adversarial content in memory affects agent behaviour
mappings.notes: Pen test report
Agent memory stores classified and protected as sensitive AI data assets — integrity verification, access controls, immutable audit log
AI system integrity verification includes memory poisoning testing — adversarial inputs designed to corrupt persistent agent state
Agent memory stores monitored for unexpected modifications — drift detection on persistent context
Agent memory stores access-controlled — only the owning agent process has write access
Enumerate all outbound data paths from agent: tools, APIs, generated output
Test whether agent can be instructed to exfiltrate via each outbound channel
Alert on unexpected data volumes in tool calls or API responses
Apply DLP controls to all agent-generated output before delivery
All agent outputs pass through content inspection before leaving system boundary

mappings.control_id: Acceptance of Extraneous Untrusted Data with Trusted Data
mappings.notes: Agent memory store accepts writes from untrusted sources alongside trusted operational knowledge
Memory content not integrity-verified before storage or retrieval
Content entering agent memory not validated before write
Vector store and memory databases with insufficient access controls permitting unauthorised writes

mappings.control_id: Multiple Releases of Same Resource or Handle
mappings.notes: Memory TTL not enforced — entries persist beyond intended lifetime
Attempt to poison memory through every channel that can write to agent memory stores
Verify that behaviour influenced by poisoned memory differs detectably from baseline
Verify memory store content integrity monitoring detects unauthorised modification

mappings.control_id: Memory store credentials leaked — attacker writes poisoned content directly
mappings.notes: Scan all paths where memory store credentials could leak — logs, config, agent context

mappings.control_id: Memory store credentials in cleartext — trivially extracted
mappings.notes: Secret manager for all memory store credentials — no cleartext anywhere

mappings.control_id: Long-lived memory store credentials enable persistent access for attacker
mappings.notes: Short-lived credentials for memory store access — rotate on each agent session

mappings.control_id: Protect agent memory stores, context databases, and shared state repositories from unauthorised read, write, and modification; enforce access controls per agent identity
mappings.control_name: PS.1.1-PS – Protect all code from unauthorised access
mappings.notes: Prevents direct tampering with agent memory and context

mappings.control_id: Maintain versioned, integrity-verified snapshots of agent memory and context stores; enable rollback to pre-poisoning states
mappings.control_name: PS.3.1-PS – Archive and protect software releases
mappings.notes: Ensures recovery capability for memory poisoning incidents

mappings.control_id: Review agent behaviour for memory-influenced anomalies — verify that persistent memory and shared context do not introduce unintended behaviour changes across sessions
mappings.control_name: PW.7.2-PS – Review the software for security vulnerabilities
mappings.notes: Catches memory poisoning effects before they propagate

mappings.control_id: When memory poisoning is detected, conduct forensic analysis to identify the poisoned records, their ingestion source, propagation path, and blast radius across agents
mappings.notes: Enables thorough incident response for memory poisoning events

mappings.control_name: Protection of Information at Rest — memory store protection
mappings.notes: Encrypt agent memory stores and context databases at rest; enforce access controls and integrity verification on all memory read and write operations

mappings.control_name: Malicious Code Protection — adversarial memory detection
mappings.notes: Extend malicious content detection to agent memory stores; detect poisoned memories, manipulated context, and adversarial state modifications
Log all agent memory operations — reads, writes, deletions — with sufficient detail to detect poisoning and support forensic investigation
Include agent memory stores, context databases, and shared state infrastructure in vulnerability scanning and security assessment

mappings.control_name: Protection and Prevention — memory integrity controls
mappings.notes: Implement security controls protecting agent memory stores from poisoning, tampering, and unauthorised modification; enforce access controls and integrity verification
Deploy detection mechanisms for agent memory manipulation; monitor for anomalous writes, content inconsistencies, and cross-session poisoning patterns
Register agent memory stores, context databases, and shared state systems in the ICT asset inventory; classify by data sensitivity

mappings.control_name: Learning and Evolving — memory poisoning post-mortem
mappings.notes: Conduct post-incident analysis for memory poisoning events; trace poisoned content to source and assess impact on agent decisions
